title: Full database reset — Vietnamese enum values stored as Mojibake
why: When importing a MySQL dump on Windows, the CMD terminal uses Windows-1252 encoding by default, not UTF-8. Without --default-character-set=utf8mb4, MySQL interprets the byte stream incorrectly. Vietnamese characters like 'chờ duyệt' (UTF-8 bytes: E1 BB 9D...) were stored as literal Windows-1252 characters, producing garbage strings like 'chß╗¥ duyß╗çt'. When Prisma tried to map these stored values to enum definitions (which are correct NFC UTF-8), it could not find a match and threw PrismaClientUnknownRequestError for every query touching OrderStatus, RollStatus, or MaterialStatus.
root_cause: MySQL dump imported without --default-character-set=utf8mb4 flag on Windows CMD; all Vietnamese enum values stored as Mojibake.
solution: Full database reset via 'npx prisma db push --force-reset'. Wipes all data and rebuilds schema from Prisma definitions with correct UTF-8 encoding. All data lost intentionally.
prevention: RULE: Always import MySQL dumps with explicit charset flag: 'mysql -u root -p --default-character-set=utf8mb4 dbname < dump.sql'. Never import via Windows CMD without this flag. Add this to startup.bat and documentation.
data_loss_note: All runtime data wiped intentionally. Schema structure preserved from Prisma schema file. No structural data loss.
